title: AD input2 plumbing for conv2d / batchnorm / layernorm / attention forward sites
goal: tensor-matmul already populates input2 (verified by ICC matmul_kernel_grad_nonzero probe). The other four kernel-style ops emit AD tape nodes with input2=null, so the backward kernel's `if (node->input2) accumulate_kernel_grad(...)` guard never fires. Wire input2 at each of the four forward sites so gradient flows through both operands. Pre-builds v1.5 LSTM/GRU and differentiable-logic kernels.
next_actions: lib/backend/tensor_conv_codegen.cpp: at the conv2d forward site, after the result tensor is built and the tape node allocated, set node->input2 = kernel_tensor before emitting the result.
Same for batchnorm (input2 = gamma), layernorm (input2 = gamma), and the four attention sites (each with the appropriate paired operand).

Verify that lib/backend/tensor_backward.cpp's existing `if (node->input2) eshkol_accumulate_tensor_grad(...)` paths fire correctly: run with a dummy upstream gradient and confirm the second-operand grad lands.
Add tests/v1_3_edge_cases/ad_input2_test.esk: numerical-gradient check for each of the four ops against finite differences. tolerance 1e-5.
acceptance: gradient of (lambda (k) (tensor-sum (conv2d image k stride))) w.r.t. k matches finite-difference within 1e-5.
Same for the attention forward op w.r.t. its key/value tensors.
Existing examples/neural_xor.esk still produces final loss < 0.001 (no regression on matmul AD path).
v1_5_payoff: v1.5 LSTM/GRU cells differentiate end-to-end. v1.5 differentiable-logic kernels (soft unification, weighted rules) build on the same tensor-backward dispatch.
